methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
SandboxAQ is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Jack Hidary, currently valued at $5.6B post-money after raising $500M to date. The peer set comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (PLTR) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer universe includes xAI, Crusoe Energy, Groq, Ayar Labs, and WEKA, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier from Series E to the next financing round, implies $7.8B. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $5.4B, with the peer set ranging from $1.1B (floor) to $250B (ceiling, xAI). The wide range reflects heterogeneity in AI & Software valuations at this stage, though the five named peers cluster between $1.6B and $10.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $4.6B (low) to $9.0B (high), with a median of $6.6B. The current $5.6B valuation sits in the lower half of this range, marginally below peer median. Key uncertainties include the durability of xAI's outlier $250B valuation and whether SandboxAQ's specific technology, market position, and growth trajectory justify positioning toward the high end of the range or consolidation toward peer median.
